title: HEALING ROOTS & KINSHIP - 211info
text: BRADLEY ANGLE HEALING ROOTS & KINSHIP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers culturally specific advocacy and support groups. Staff connect survivors to resources for housing, employment, education, medical care, legal assistance, and other services. Helps survivors learn more about domestic violence, get support from staff and peers, and create social connections within an affirming and welcoming space. Eligibility: Black and African-American survivors of domestic violence Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm, appointment required Email (503) 232-1528 311 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 232-1528 ext 311 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and intake FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus lines 4 and 72 stop at N Killingsworth Street and N Albina Avenue Services: Domestic Violence Support Groups * African American Community Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING ASSISTANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - MARTHA'S FOLX MONEY MANAGEMENT - ECONOMIC EMPOWERMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
